title: Simple Harmonic Motion
learningObjectives: Define simple harmonic motion and its characteristics
Use x = A cos(ωt) to describe SHM
Calculate velocity and acceleration in SHM
introText: Simple Harmonic Motion (SHM) is the oscillatory motion where the restoring force is proportional to and directed opposite to the displacement. It describes the motion of pendulums, springs, and many wave phenomena.

steps.heading: What is SHM?
steps.body:
SHM occurs when the restoring force is proportional to displacement:

F = −kx (Hooke's Law)

This gives: a = −ω²x

Key terms:
• Amplitude (A): maximum displacement from equilibrium
• Period (T): time for one complete oscillation
• Frequency (f): number of oscillations per second (f = 1/T)
• Angular frequency (ω): ω = 2πf = 2π/T
steps.deepDive: The negative sign in a = −ω²x is crucial — it means acceleration is always directed back toward the equilibrium position, which is what makes the motion oscillatory.

steps.heading: Equations of SHM
steps.body:
Displacement: x = A cos(ωt)
Velocity: v = −Aω sin(ωt)
Acceleration: a = −Aω² cos(ωt) = −ω²x

Maximum values:
• Maximum displacement: A (at t = 0)
• Maximum velocity: Aω (at equilibrium, x = 0)
• Maximum acceleration: Aω² (at maximum displacement)

For a spring-mass system: ω = √(k/m)
For a simple pendulum: ω = √(g/L)
steps.deepDive: The phase relationships are important: velocity is maximum when displacement is zero (equilibrium) and vice versa. Acceleration and displacement are exactly out of phase (180° apart).

steps.heading: Energy in SHM
steps.body:
Total mechanical energy is constant in SHM:
E_total = ½kA² = ½mω²A²

At displacement x:
KE = ½m(Aω)² − ½mω²x² = ½mω²(A² − x²)
PE = ½mω²x²

At equilibrium (x = 0): all energy is kinetic
At maximum displacement (x = ±A): all energy is potential
steps.deepDive: Energy oscillates between KE and PE at twice the frequency of the displacement. If you plot KE and PE versus time, they are both sinusoidal but always sum to a constant E_total.

steps.heading: SHM Visualiser
steps.instructionText: Adjust amplitude and angular frequency. Watch the oscillation and note how the period and maximum speed change.

steps.questionText: A mass on a spring oscillates with amplitude 0.1 m and angular frequency 5 rad/s. What is its maximum speed?
steps.explanation: Maximum speed = Aω = 0.1 × 5 = 0.5 m/s (occurs at equilibrium position).

questions.questionText: A pendulum has a period of 2 seconds. What is its angular frequency?
